title: couepia guianensis subsp. glandulosa
intro: Couepia guianensis subsp. glandulosa, also known as the Couepia tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Chrysobalanaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can be attributed to the presence of various compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Couepia guianensis subsp. glandulosa include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat wounds, as well as its ability to act as an antioxidant and protect against oxidative stress. Herbal preparations of Couepia guianensis subsp. glandulosa are typically made from its bark, leaves, or roots, and are often used to create teas, infusions, or tinctures that can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
